prose: Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The finite-difference Fukui approach used N, N+1, and N−1 electronic states (cation: charge +1 doublet; anion: charge −1 doublet) with Mulliken partial charges. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). All three SCF calculations converged cleanly; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true. Charge conservation: Σ(f+) = 1.0000, Σ(f−) = 1.0000. The headline value reported is the f+ (electrophilic Fukui index) for atom 1 (N), which is the most electrophilic site at f+ = 0.48422572. Full per-atom results (f+, f−, f0, dual descriptor) are tabulated in the report above. No warnings were raised by the engine.
